content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO BOX 1175 • SACRAMENTO 95814 • (916) JOVE BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

April 3, 1978

Cher McIntyre
Executive Secretary
5261 E. Beverly Blvd.
Los Angeles, California 90022

Dear Ms. McIntyre:

This letter will confirm our conversation of March 31, 1978, in which I advised you that Assemblyman Torres must make a good faith estimate of the value of the four passes mentioned in Ms. Todd's letter of March 1, 1978. In determining value, the Act requires the filer to estimate the fair market value of the gift at the time it is received. It is important to note that the value of tickets or passes does not hinge upon whether they are used. In order to assist Assemblyman Torres in amending his Statement of Economic Interests, I have enclosed an opinion issued by the Commission regarding the reporting of free tickets and passes.

Lastly, as Ms. Todd indicated in her conversation with you, a parking pass from the Museum of Science and Industry is not reportable if it is used solely in connection with official business. See the enclosed opinion issued to Assemblyman Thomas.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to call me.

Sincerely,

Ted Brim
Chief, Conflicts of
Interest Division

TP:mfa
Enclosures

Michael Bennett                    -2-                    March 15, 1978

     When we explained that the entries included services that, if used,
could be of a wide range of values, we were informed that the FPPC
was aware of this problem, but it had no regulation to cover it at
present. As we understood the Hopkins decision, it deals with
"Fair Market Value" - not with items that have no intrinsic value
unless used. It was also explained to Ms. Todd, that estimates
had been made on previous occasions as required, and the resulting
criticism from the press of various public officials for allegedly
accepting gifts from various entities, when in fact no gift was
received at all, made the information suspect; and at best, less
than accurate and informative. Therefore, because of the misuse
of said information under penalty of perjury (See page 1 of Form
701), it would seem ill-advised at the very least, to report deli-
berately misleading and falsified information.

     We are well aware of the difficulty of enforcing reporting
requirements and keeping the public, as well as State agencies,
informed on a given legislators activities. However, to require
that the legislator deliberately file contrived and erroneous in-
formation and then to make such information a matter of public
record, is irresponsible and does not fulfill the intent of the
Political Reform Act of 1974. Therefore, we must question this
practice/requirement, and so as to fully comply with disclosure
requirements, we ask you to consider this situation carefully, so
that we may have a comprehensive decision as to how the reporting
should take place rather than operating in a vacuum of information.

     Rather than request an opinion from the Attorney General's
Office, we would like to request that the FPPC consider the regu-
lations regarding the worth of services offered but not afforded,
and advise subsequent to study.

     Thank you for your time and consideration.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        CHER McINTYRE
                                        Executive Secretary

CM/sg

Enclosures

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                322-5901              322 6441

March 1, 1978

Honorable Art Torres
Assemblyman
5261 East Beverly Blvd.
Los Angeles, CA 90022

Dear Mr. Torres:

RE: Statement of Economic Interests Executed
     February 27, 1978

A review of your Statement indicates it does not conform in certain respects with the filing requirements of the Political Reform Act. The box(es) checked below indicate items that are incomplete or incorrect and may require clarification.

[ ] COVER PAGE
    [ ] Verification
    [ ] Period Covered (It is unclear whether the Statement you filed disclosed your interests for the correct period. You should complete a new cover sheet to indicate what the correct period is. If your Statement does not accurately report your interests for the correct period, you should complete any necessary schedules. Please complete the enclosed "Supplemental Cover Sheet" and, if necessary, "Supplemental Schedules 721-A through G.")

[ ] SCHEDULE A – INVESTMENTS
    For the investment(s) indicated below, the following information was not included:
    [ ] Nature of Interest    [ ] General Description of Business Activity    [ ] Value of Interest


[ ] SCHEDULE B – INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY
    For the interest(s) in real property indicated below, the following information was not included:
    [ ] Address or Other Precise Location    [ ] Nature of Interest    [ ] Value of Interest


[ ] SCHEDULE C – INVESTMENTS AND INTERESTS IN REAL PROPERTY HELD BY BUSINESS ENTITIES YOU
    CONTROL
    [ ] Should be completed for business entity(ies) indicated below which were listed on Schedule 721-A if you own a 50% or greater interest.

    [ ] Investments Held by Business Entity
        For the investment(s) indicated below, the following information was not included:
        [ ] Nature of Interest    [ ] General Description of Business Activity    [ ] Value of Interest

[ ] Interests in Real Property Held by Business Entity
For the interest(s) in real property indicated below, the following information was not included:
[ ] Address or Other Precise Location       [ ] Nature of Interest       [ ] Value of Interest


[ ] SCHEDULE D – INCOME
[ ] It appears you may have received income from the following source(s) which was not reported on this schedule.
Please complete the enclosed supplemental schedule if you received income of $250 or more from this source(s):


For the source(s) of income indicated below, the following information was not included:
[ ] Address of Source       [ ] Business Activity       [ ] Description of Consideration       [ ] Value


[x] SCHEDULE E – GIFTS
For the gift(s) indicated below, the following information was not included:
[ ] Donor's Name              [ ] Address of Donor              [ ] Business Activity of Donor
[ ] Date of Gift              [x] Value of Gift                 [ ] Description of Gift

a.   Del Mar Thoroughbred Club                                d.   Los Angeles Turf Club –
b.   California Museum of Science & Industry                       Clubhouse pass
c.   East Los Angeles athletic & social pass

[ ] SCHEDULE F – INCOME OF BUSINESS ENTITY WHICH PROVIDES LEGAL OR BROKERAGE SERVICES
It appears that you or your spouse may own, directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10% or greater interest in a business
entity which provides legal or brokerage services. If that is true, you are required to disclose the names of the clients who
paid fees to the business entity of which your pro rata share was $1,000 or more. The business entity is:

[ ] SCHEDULE G – INCOME OF BUSINESS ENTITIES WHICH DO NOT PROVIDE LEGAL OR BROKERAGE SERVICES
It appears that you or your spouse may own, directly, indirectly or beneficially, a 10% or greater interest in a business
entity which does not provide legal or brokerage services. If that is true, you are required to disclose the names of the
clients who paid fees to the business entity of which your pro rata share was $10,000 or more. The business entity is:

[ ] THE FOLLOWING SCHEDULE(S) WERE NOT INCLUDED:

Enclosed are the Supplemental Schedules which should be completed. Complete instructions regarding reportable interests
and the manner of reporting is contained on the reverse side of the schedule. If you have nothing to report on the
schedule(s), please indicate "none." When you have completed the forms please send the original to your agency which
will make and retain a copy and forward the original to this office.

If you have any questions regarding the completion of the forms, please contact this office.


Sincerely,

[signature]
Gina Todd
Documents Examiner

cc: Clerk of the Assembly

Reply to:
Sacramento Address
State Capitol
Sacramento 95814
Telephone: (916) 445-1670

District Address
5261 East Beverly Boulevard
Los Angeles, CA 90022
Telephone: (213) 728-8882

Bob Morales
Administrative Assistant

COMMITTEE
HEARING

Assembly

California Legislature
ART TORRES
ASSEMBLYMAN, FIFTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

Subcommittees on
Mental Health and
Developmental Disabilities

Judiciary
Ways and Means
Joint Committee on
Job Development

BELL BELL GARDENS, BELVEDERE, BOYLE HEIGHTS, CHINATOWN,
COMMERCE, DOWNTOWN LOS ANGELES, EAST LOS ANGELES,
LITTLE TOKYO, MARAVILLA, MAYWOOD,
MONTEBELLO PARK, VERNON

CHAIRMAN
WAYS AND MEANS SUBCOMMITTEE ON HEALTH AND WELFARE

March 15, 1978

Michael Bennett, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "R" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: Statement of Economic Interests, Executed 2-27-78.

Dear Mr. Bennett:

In accordance with the Political Reform Act of 1974,
our committee filed a Form 721, Statement of Economic
Interests, on behalf of Assemblyman Art Torres for 1977.
Included in that statement were four (4) items of services
offered to the Assemblyman which were never used by him
nor anyone else. So as to comply within the context of
"full disclosure," the items were listed as gifts but as
they were never used, the exact value was not stated.
The entries on Form 721 included names, addresses, services
offered and a notation that the value of such gifts were
concluded to be above $25 but that an exact amount was not
determined because the service was never used.

After the filing deadline, our committee received a
letter from the FPPC, copy enclosed, stating that an exact
value must be determined on an amended, supplemental statement. Upon contacting Gina Todd of the FPPC, we were
informed that a "good faith estimate" must be put on services mentioned. When we questioned the rationale behind
putting a value on a service offered but not rendered,
and that the nature of the entries was such that their
value would be determined only on the basis of their use
(at the same time taking care to disclose offer), we
were informed that the amount could be any amount but that
some amount must be reported. In speaking with sources
of gifts, we were repeatedly informed that an exact value
could not be determined if offer was not accepted for use.

NOT PRINTED OR MAILED AT PUBLIC EXPENSE
